sales.description:
ITALY. Lucania. Thourioi. AR Distater (Double Nomos) (15.76 gms), ca. 400-350 B.C. NGC Ch F, Strike: 5/5 Surface: 4/5. Fine Style.
HGC-1, 1256; HN Italy-1805. Obverse: Head of Athena left, wearing helmeted decorated with Skylla; Reverse: Bull butting right; in exergue, fish right. While some circulation has softened the high points, this Distater retains lovely surface quality and cabinet tone. A pleasing example of the type.

Estimate: 800 - 1200...

sales.description:
ITALY. Lucania. Thourioi. AR Stater (Nomos) (7.63 gms), ca. 400-350 B.C. NGC Ch VF, Strike: 3/5 Surface: 3/5. Marks, Flan Flaw.
HGC-1, 1260. Obverse: Helmeted head of Athena right; Reverse: Bull butting right; in exergue, fish right; all within incuse circle. A deeply toned specimen, though some roughness is observed upon each side.

Estimate: 400 - 600...
